metadata.name: 1.2.b Places in stationary social welfare facilities (including branches) per 10 thous. inhabitants
metadata.priority: Enabling real and full social integration of persons with disabilities and psychological, social, functional and economic support for their families
metadata.definition: Number of places in stationary social welfare facilities (including branches) per 10 thous. inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: The data about stationary social welfare institutions is collected using the PS-03 report.The report concerns stationary social welfare institutions and selected support centers with 24-hour periodic stay, eg an community self-help home (form of institutional support for people with mental disorders) as of 31 December.Social welfare home provides living, care, support and educational services at the level of the applicable standard, in the scope and forms resulting from individual needs of people who are staying there. Staying in social walfare home is payable.Depending on for whom the facilities are intended, they are divided into homes for: aged persons, chronically ill with somatic disorders, chronically mentally ill, mentally retarded, children and youth mentally retarded, physically handicapped, alcohol addicts.The voivode keeps a register of social welfare homes and announces it in the voivodship official journal.Family-based assistance houses - provides help for people who needs 24-hour support and care services due to their age.Facilities guaranting 24-hour care for persons with disabilities, chronically ill or aged persons. The facilities operating on the basis of regulations on business or statutory activity. After obtaining the permit of the voivode the facilities provides care and living services. The voivode keeps a register of these facilities.Other facilities include institutions such as: family support center, support center for victims of domestic violence, crisis intervention center - these facilities provides support, including shelter for persons and families in a state of crisis.
